scope.selection_note: Codex-only prep for ROUTE and CORRIDOR duplicates from current data/canonical/routes.jsonl, data/canonical/corridors.jsonl, data/identity-overrides/merges.json, data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json, and read-only validateRepo duplicate_global_identity output. Selection is intentionally conservative: route service variants, compound routes, adjacent or only partially overlapping corridors, stale ids, existing aliases, targets that are aliases, aliases that are merge targets, and do-not-merge hits were excluded.

validator_evidence: validateRepo duplicate_global_identity: Global corridor records corridor_bay-parkway-avenue-j-to-shore-pkwy and corridor_bay-parkway-cropsey share identity key corridor_bay-parkway.

validateRepo duplicate_global_identity: Global corridor records corridor_bay-parkway-avenue-j-to-shore-pkwy and corridor_bay-parkway-cropsey share identity key corridor_bay-parkway-from-ave-j-to-shore-parkway.

proposed_aliases.reason: The alias is the Bay Parkway segment of the same Bay Parkway/Cropsey Avenue bus-priority study area. This is not a same-street-only collision: current canonical evidence and multiple local source blocks define the same study area with Bay Parkway from Avenue J to Shore Parkway and Cropsey Avenue from Bay Parkway to 26th Avenue, while the target already carries the exact Bay Parkway limit alias and merged Bay Parkway segment values.

guards.partial_overlap_guard: accepted because source evidence identifies one Bay Parkway/Cropsey Avenue study area and the target carries the exact Bay Parkway Avenue J to Shore Parkway identity alias and merged limits

validator_evidence: validateRepo duplicate_global_identity: Global corridor records corridor_bay-parkway-cropsey and corridor_cropsey-avenue-bay-pkwy-to-26th-ave share identity key corridor_cropsey-ave.

proposed_aliases.reason: The alias is the Cropsey Avenue segment of the same Bay Parkway/Cropsey Avenue bus-priority study area. Source blocks for both records use the same paired study-area definition, and the target already carries the exact Cropsey Avenue Bay Parkway-to-26th Avenue identity alias and merged values.

guards.partial_overlap_guard: accepted because source evidence identifies one Bay Parkway/Cropsey Avenue study area and the target carries the exact Cropsey Avenue Bay Parkway to 26th Avenue identity alias and merged limits

proposal_guards: Each proposed alias_id and target_id exists in current canonical JSONL.
No proposed alias_id is already present as a merge alias in current data/identity-overrides/merges.json.
No proposed alias_id is currently a merge target in current data/identity-overrides/merges.json.
No proposed target_id is currently a merge alias in current data/identity-overrides/merges.json.
No proposed pair appears in current data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json.
No stale ids, alias==target pairs, route variants, compound routes, or ambiguous same-street-only corridor collisions were included.
excluded_current_duplicate_classes: Excluded all current route duplicate directions because they were local/limited/SBS lifecycle or compound-route traps without sufficiently decisive source evidence for this batch's conservative route-variant guard.
Excluded corridor_125th-st-manhattan -> corridor_125th-st-crosstown-brt-phase2 because the only guard-safe direction points from a richer current corridor to a broad BRT Phase II candidate target.

Excluded Broadway, Church Avenue, Cross Bay Boulevard, Flatlands Avenue, Kings Highway, Lexington Avenue, Montauk Branch, Third Avenue, Utica Avenue, Webster/Third Avenue, and Woodhaven Boulevard collisions where the current evidence shows adjacent limits, nested segments, borough conflicts, phase-candidate context, or broad street-name-only overlap.
Excluded corridor_bay-parkway-cropsey as an alias to either segment-specific target because that would collapse the combined study-area record into only one of its two named study-area components.

command_evidence.command: bun --eval 'import { validateRepo } from "./packages/pipeline/src/validate.ts"; ... print duplicate_global_identity route/corridor issues'
command_evidence.purpose: Read-only local validateRepo inspection; observed 537 duplicate_global_identity issues total and 57 route/corridor duplicate_global_identity messages.

command_evidence.command: bun --eval '<read current route/corridor canonical JSONL, current merges, current do-not-merge, and validateRepo issues; print guard-safe directions>'
command_evidence.purpose: Checked stale ids, existing aliases, aliases that are merge targets, targets that are aliases, alias==target, and do-not-merge hits before selecting proposals.
Inspected canonical payloads, record_aliases, source_ids, merged field values, and evidence refs for selected Bay Parkway/Cropsey candidates.
Read local source blocks for Bay Parkway/Cropsey source evidence quotes without running provider-backed commands.
Checked prior prep and cleanup notes for stale/already-applied route/corridor aliases and known exclusions.

blockers: A final parallel validateRepo spot-check attempted after the successful validator inspection hit a transient SQLite busy lock; no data was written by that failed read-only command, and the selected proposals are anchored to the earlier successful validateRepo output plus canonical/source evidence.
